case_title: JOSHUA DODD VS. LARAMAR SF URBAN APARTMENTS et al

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501,PLAINTIFF JOSHUA DODD'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSE AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE RESPONSES TO DEMAND FOR INSPECTION/PRODUCTION FOR DOCUMENTS- SET TWO AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES-SET ONE WITHOUT OBJECTIONS BY PERSONAL SERVICE WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES- SET ONE, SPECIFICALLY TO REQUESTS 12.7; 15.1; 16.1; 16.2; 16.3; 16.4; 16.5; 16.6; 16.7; 16.8; 16.9; AND 16.10 BY PERSONAL SERVICE WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO PAY $2,125 WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED AS OPEN COURT. JUDGE HARRY M. DORFMAN, CLERK N. BAUTISTA, NOT REPORTED, (501)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Response And Further Responses To Discovery Req.'S And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Jaimie Bombard (415-861-5718) appearing for Plaintiff and moving party/ No other apperances.

Tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JOSHUA DODD'S Motion For Order Compelling Response And Further Responses To Discovery Requests And For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Defendants shall provide responses to Demand for Inspection/Production for Documents- Set Two and Special Interrogatories-Set One without objections by personal service within 5 days of Notice of Entry of Order. Defendants shall provide verified responses to Form Interrogatories- Set One, specifically to requests 12.7; 15.1; 16.1; 16.2; 16.3; 16.4; 16.5; 16.6; 16.7; 16.8; 16.9; and 16.10 by personal service within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Defendants and their counsel are ordered to pay $2,125 within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Order for the same signed as open court. Judge Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk N. Bautista, Not Reported, (501)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Continued To Oct-08-13 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 210

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Response And Further Responses To Discovery Req.'S And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Continued To Sep-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JOSHUA DODD's Motion For Order Compelling Response And Further Responses To Discovery Req.'S And For Monetary Sanctions is continued to September 20, 2013. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers no later than September 4, 2013 by 4 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date.
